question: Under Australian road safety guidelines, if you are involved in a collision with a marsupial (such as a kangaroo or wombat), what is the recommended action regarding the animal's pouch?

answers.text: You must immediately transport the animal to the nearest vet
Check the pouch for a living joey, but only if it is safe to do so
It is illegal to touch the animal until a ranger arrives
You are required by law to move the animal at least 50 metres from the road

question: In Western Australia, is it an offence to transport more than what quantity of potatoes without being a member of a Potato Corporation?
In South Australia, how much distance must you maintain when following a Long Vehicle (over 7.5m) outside a built-up area?
Under the Australian Road Rules, you may only flash your high-beam headlights to warn other drivers in which specific scenario?

answers.text: To warn of a speed camera
To warn of a police Random Breath Test

answers.text: No, only built-in Global Positioning Systems are allowed

question: Under the National Road Rules, if you are leading a mob of sheep at night, what light must you carry at the front?

What does a Give Way sign require you to do in Australia?

answers.text: Slow down and give way to all traffic

question: If a funeral procession is passing in New South Wales, what is the specific rule regarding breaking the line of the procession?

answers.text: Speed up to pass before it crosses
Stop and wait for it to cross, as hitting one can cause serious vehicle damage
